rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF PATRICIA MADAUHS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Platte River Insurance Company'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment Against Bond Principal Paula R. Bibbs
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 2] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Michael B. McNaughton. Before the court is Platte River Insurance Company's Motion for Judgment Against Bond Principal Paula R. Bibbs ("Motion"). The Motion requests (1) Judgment be entered in favor of Platte River Insurance Company and against Paula R. Bibbs in the amount of $361,123.13, pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure sections 882 and 883; (2) an order that the surcharge judgment entered by this court against Paula R. Bibbs is satisfied, in the amount of $361,123.13; and (3) an order canceling the probate bond previously entered in this matter pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 995.430, and releasing Platte River Insurance Company from liability on the bond pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure sections 996.110 and 996.120. No opposition to the Motion has been filed. The Motion is GRANTED, as follows: On May 24, 2021, Paula Bibbs as then-conservator of the estate of Patricia Madauhs, filed Additional Bond of Conservator, in the amount of $1,473,000, executed by surety Platte River Insurance Company ("Platte River"). Ms. Bibbs was appointed conservator of the person and estate of Patrica Madauhs on April 3, 2017, with initial bond of $27,000 executed by Platte River, and Letters issued the same day. Ms. Bibbs was removed as conservator of the person and estate of Patricia Madauhs by order of this court, on February 1, 2024. San Francisco Public Guardian ("SFPG") was appointed successor conservator of person and estate on February 2, 2024 and Letters issued the same day. On October 28, 2025, this court entered Order Approving Settlement of Objections to Third and Final Account and Report of Former Conservator Paula Bibbs, and Petition for Surcharge. The Order authorized Conservator SFPG to enter into the attached Mutual Release and Settlement Agreement, and dismissed Conservator's objections to Third and Final Account and Report of Former Conservator Paula Bibbs. As part of the Settlement, the parties agreed that a surcharge order should be entered against former conservator Paula Bibbs in the amount of $361,123.13, and that surety Platte River would be obligated by the surcharge order to pay that amount to SFPG as Conservator. The parties further agreed Platte River could move the court for an order compelling Ms. Bibbs to repay that amount, plus any court ordered interest, under Code of Civil Procedure sections 882 and 883, based on Platte River's liability as surety on the surcharge order. On November 6, 2026, successor conservator SFPG filed Receipt of Payment Per Settlement, acknowledging receipt from Platte River, as the surety on the conservatorship bond, the amount of $361,543.94 for payment per terms of the Settlement. On November 20, this Motion was filed. On December 5, 2025, this court entered Order Settling Third and Final Account and Report of Former Conservator; Approving Fees and Costs Advanced to Attorney Conservator; and Discharging Bond. The order stated at paragraph 3, "Bond is approved for discharge after the filing of the appropriate receipts [regarding payment of court-ordered legal fees and costs]." On December 11, 2025, Rowena Navia filed Receipt of Attorney for the Former Conservator Paula R. Bibbs for Payment of Fees and Costs.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF PATRICIA MADAUHS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR For The Purpose Of Posting Tentative Ruling
rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 2] Under Code of Civil Procedure section 882(b), when a judgment is based on an obligation of one judgment debtor as surety for another, and the surety satisfies all or part of the judgment, the surety may compel repayment from the principal. The judgment debtor that is entitled to compel repayment under Section 882 may bring a noticed motion for an order determining liability for contribution or repayment, after the judgment creditor is paid in full and has no other outstanding claim, and the judgment debtor's obligation has been fully extinguished. (Code of Civil Proc. sec. 883(a); Comerica Bank v Runyon (2017) 16 CA5th 473, 482.) On the record before the court, Platte River Insurance Company, as surety for Ms. Bibbs, has paid the surcharge amount in full to the Conservator pursuant to the Settlement Agreement, and the Conservator has acknowledged that payment. SFPG has no other outstanding claim. Therefore, pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure sections 882 and 883, Paula R. Bibbs, as former conservator of the person and estate of Patricia Madauhs, is hereby ORDERED to pay to Platte River Insurance Company the amount of $361,543.94, within 30 days of service of Notice of Entry of Order. Under Code of Civil Procedure section 996.110(a), "A surety on a bond given in an action or proceeding may at any time apply to the court for an order that the surety be released from liability on the bond." Under Code of Civil Procedure section 996.120, "the court shall determine whether injury to the beneficiary would result from substitution or release of the surety," and "shall order the release of the surety" if the court determines the release would not reduce the amount of the bond below the statutory minimum. Here, the successor conservator, SFPG, is a government agency exempt from the statutory minimum bond requirement (see, Probate Code secs. 2320, 2922(b)). The court finds the beneficiary of the bond Platte River executed for Ms. Bibbs when she was conservator of the estate in this matter - i.e., the conservatee, Patricia Madauhs - would not be harmed if Platte River was released as surety on the bond. This court has already ordered the bond discharged pursuant to the terms of the December 5, 2025 Order Settling Third and Final Account and Report of Former Conservator; Approving Fees and Costs Advanced to Attorney Conservator; and Discharging Bond. Those terms appear to have been fulfilled. Platte River is released from liability on the bond. Movant Platte River Insurance Company shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOSE ALFREDO ZERMENO II
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required for the judicial officer to review and determine whether this petition should be taken off calendar, as nothing has been filed to address the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 09/19/2025) since this petition was initially set for 10/06/2025: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. The caption must begin with "Order for Final Distribution". LRSF 14.35(I)(6)(b). 2) No DE-120 form is on file. A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the form. 3) Paragraph 13 of petition: The date of giving notice to the Franchise Tax Board must be stated. LRSF 14.35(I)(2). 4) Paragraph 30: Liabilities of the estate did not include additional fees and costs of the guardian ad litem ($3,309.20) sought to be approved. 5) Paragraph 32: No waiver of account signed by Giovanni Zermeno as an adult is on file. Although the guardian ad litem previously signed a waiver of account, the order that appointed the guardian ad litem did not authorize the waiver without prior court approval. 6) Paragraph 34: Petitioner's address change has not been reported to the Court (by filing an amended DE-147 form or MC-040 form). 7) Paragraph 37: No Exhibit G (schedule of proposed distribution) was attached. 8) Paragraph 40: i) Detailed schedules of receipts, gains, and losses must be filed to request statutory fees based on any amount other than the inventory and appraisal value, even if the account is waived. CRC 7.550(b)(6) & 7.705(b). ii) Please explain why the inventory and appraisal value of each car should not be deducted as a loss. 9) Paragraph 41: No statement required by CRC 7.702 has been filed in support of the attorney's additional fees for extraordinary services. 10) Paragraph 42: No Exhibit G was attached. 11) Exhibit B: i) The complete legal description of the estate real property on hand must be provided. CRC 7.651(b)(3). Note: The APN appears incorrect. Should it be Block 4939, Lot 009B (not 0098)? ii) The estate cash on hand is insufficient to pay fees and costs sought to be approved by this petition (and the guardian ad litem's). Unless the beneficiaries have advanced sufficient cash to the estate to create the source of payment required by Probate Code section 10831(d)(fees and costs must be ordered to be paid "out of the estate"), liens must be imposed on the estate real property sought to be distributed in kind, with or without 5% simple annual interest available under LRSF 14.56, to secure court-approved fees and costs. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LARRY DEAN COATE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required for the judicial officer to review and determine whether this petition should be taken off calendar, as nothing has been filed to address the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 10/07/2025): 1) The caption of the proposed order must begin with "Order for Final Distribution". LRSF 14.35(I)(6)(b). 2) Re the DE-120 form filed on 10/01/2025: i) Item 1 must specify petitioner's representative capacity (executor). ii) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the form at least 15 days before the hearing. iii) Arithinat Surawatthana's mailing address was not listed on the proof of service. No consent to electronic service (EFS-005-CV form) is on file. 3) Re paragraph 7 of petition & Exhibit 2: The issues re petitioner's creditor claim for funeral expenses must be addressed. See notes e-mailed on 09/23/2025 re petitioner's drop-off ex parte petition (DE-174 form). 4) Re paragraph 21: Receipts representing refunds of disbursements from the estate must be deducted from the statutory fee base receipts. The statutory fees must be recalculated and reallocated between the former and current attorneys, and the former attorney's new consent to his portion must be filed. 5) Re paragraph 25: i) Please explain why extraordinary fees for ancillary probate in Thailand should be charged to this domiciliary estate and why such fees should not be paid out of property of ancillary probate in Thailand distributable to a specific devisee. ii) Please explain why the attorney's services for "Marshalling" and "Defense of will" should not be characterized as ordinary services. 6) Re paragraph 26 & Exhibit 1: "Accounting Invoice" ($715.50) must be deducted from petitioner or her attorney's statutory fees, as preparation of an account is part of ordinary services. LRSF 14.51 & 14.53. 7) Re paragraph 29: An allegation must also be made as to whether any beneficiary (defined to include devisees) is currently or has previously been confined in any correctional facility described in Probate Code section 9202(b). 8) Re Exhibit A: Selling costs totaled $108,637.16 including $30,000 "Attorney Fees & Court Costs", but the schedule of disbursements only reported $78,637.16 as selling costs, and the $30,000 attorney fees and costs were unaccounted for. 9) Re schedule of additional property received: If the decedent held Redwood and San Francisco Federal credit union accounts, each account must be inventoried and appraised by petitioner as of the date of death. Each interest payment made after the date of death must be separately reported and added to the schedule of receipts. If the decedent did not hold those credit union accounts, please explain how the estate became entitled to collect funds from each account. 10) Re schedule of receipts: Please explain why no dividends were paid from Morgan Stanley. 11) Re schedule of disbursements: "Toll Bridge Invoice" ($7) is absorbed as part of statutory fees. LRSF 14.57(B). 12) Re schedule of losses: "Foreign Money" must be appraised by the probate referee. 13) Re schedule of property on hand at end of account period: i) A statement of each financial account covering 03/31/2025 must be filed. LRSF 14.34(D). ii) A declaration re retention of original account statements and inspection thereof required by LRSF 14.59(A) & CRC 2.257 must be filed. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.
